relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Safely through the sea, they learn the island is Malta.
Unusual kindness meets them in rain and cold.
A viper fastens to Paul’s hand from the firewood.
The islanders read misfortune as Justice catching a murderer.
Paul shakes the creature into the fire and remains unharmed.
Expectation of death turns into mistaken divinization.
Publius hosts them kindly for three days.
Paul prays, lays hands, and heals Publius’ father.
The island’s weak come and are healed.
Honor and needed supplies are given for the next voyage.
After winter, an Alexandrian ship carries them onward.
Syracuse receives them for three days.
The route moves by Rhegium to Puteoli.
Siblings are found, hospitality is given, and Rome draws near.
Roman siblings come to meet Paul; seeing them, he gives thanks and takes courage.
In Rome, Paul is guarded yet allowed his own lodging.
He calls the Judean leaders and explains his innocence toward people and customs.
Roman examiners found no death-cause.
Appeal to Caesar came without accusation against his nation.
The chain is for the hope of Israel.
They have received no harmful report about him.
They are willing to hear about the opposed Way.
From morning to evening he witnesses the Divine reign and persuades from Moses and Prophets concerning Jesus.
Some are persuaded, others do not trust.
Discord meets Isaiah’s Spirit-spoken word.
Hearing without understanding and seeing without perceiving are named.
Closed eyes, heavy ears, and thick heart resist turning and healing.
The salvation/healing of the Divine is sent to the nations, and they will hear.
A later manuscript tradition adds continued dispute after departure.
For two years Paul welcomes all who come.
Acts ends with the reign proclaimed and Jesus taught boldly, unhindered.
